title: Xylophyllos cupressiformis

Xylophyllum cupressiforme, also known as Xylophyllum, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It is a shrub native to Mexico. 

Xylophyllum cupressiforme has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Xylophyllum cupressiforme include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Xylophyllum cupressiforme include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
